---
title: "Erosive Burning"
subtitle: "Effects of High Port Velocity on Burning Rate"
order: 7
---

## Introduction

In the standard 0D internal ballistics model, the burning rate depends only on chamber pressure via Vieille’s law \( r = a P_c^n \).

However, when hot combustion gases flow at high velocity parallel to the burning surface, they can significantly increase the local burn rate. This is called **erosive burning**. It is most severe near the aft end of the grain where mass flux is highest.

## Lenoir-Robillard Model

The total burn rate is modeled as:

$$
r = r_0 + r_e
$$

where \( r_0 = a P_c^n \) and the erosive term is:

$$
r_e = \frac{\alpha G^{0.8}}{D^{0.2}} \exp\left( -\beta \frac{\rho_p r}{G} \right)
$$

- \( G = \dot{m} / A_p \): local mass flux (kg/m²·s)
- \( D \): hydraulic diameter of the port (m)
- \( \alpha, \beta \): propellant-specific constants

## Effect on Mass Generation

When erosive burning is active, the mass generation rate becomes:

$$
\dot{m}_\text{gen} = \rho_p A_b(w) \, r(P_c, G)
$$

This term is used in the chamber pressure differential equation from Chapter 5.

## Summary

Erosive burning is an important correction for long or high-performance motors. It is usually added after the basic 0D model is working.

The next chapter discusses nozzle flow and thrust.

:::{.callout-note}
Future Python code in `code/simulator.py` will support an `erosive=True` option.
:::
